narrative.summary:
📍 Bosna Express
66-99 Fresh Pond Rd, Queens, NY 11385, USA

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're under the elevated M train on Fresh Pond Road, where the tracks cast moving shadows across the sidewalk. The restaurant sits at street level below the station platform—utilitarian tiles, fluorescent lighting, and a counter setup built for speed. Through the windows, you'll see that unusually wide platform overhead, the one everyone mentions because subway platforms in Queens are usually crammed.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: This is Balkan fast food: ćevapi (those skinless beef sausages), pljeskavica patties, and proper lepinja bread that's somewhere between pita and flatbread. You're looking at plates under $12, the kind of food that makes sense when you're catching the M train and need something that actually fills you up. The somun comes hot, which matters more than you'd think.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐ "This is a typical New York City subway station that was renovated not too long ago. It is only served by the M line. (The M to Metropolitan is very sporadic during off-peak hours). There is some kitschy art murals on the walls as well as token booth and some public seating. It is also a relatively safe station."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "The platform is SO WIDE, which is great. Also the station is nearly litter free. There's lots of great places to eat below the station."

⭐⭐⭐⭐ "It's an outdoor station that is covered in places to stay out of the rain. The decking isn't totally dallying apart but will be in another year or two."
